message:
feat(ffs-mvcc): implement Serializable Snapshot Isolation (SSI) with rw-antidependency detection

Add full SSI conflict detection on top of the existing FCW (First-Committer-Wins)
layer. SSI detects dangerous rw-antidependency cycles — the classic "write skew"
anomaly that FCW cannot prevent — by tracking read sets and checking for
concurrent writes that invalidated them.

Transaction read tracking:
- Add `reads: BTreeMap<BlockNumber, CommitSeq>` to Transaction for recording
  which blocks (and at what version) were read during the transaction lifetime
- `record_read(block, version_seq)`: Records a read; first-read-wins semantics
  so repeated reads of the same block don't overwrite the original version
- `read_set()` and `write_set()` accessors for SSI validation

SSI conflict detection:
- New `CommitError::SsiConflict` variant with pivot_block, read_version,
  write_version, and concurrent_txn for precise diagnostics
- `CommittedTxnRecord`: Retains txn_id, commit_seq, snapshot, write_set, and
  read_set for bidirectional antidependency checking after commit
- `MvccStore::commit_ssi()`: Validates against SSI log — for each block in
  the committing txn's read set, checks if any concurrent committed txn wrote
  that block after the reader's snapshot; read-only txns are exempt
- `MvccStore::ssi_log`: Append-only log of committed txn records
- `MvccStore::prune_ssi_log(threshold)`: Prunes entries with commit_seq <=
  threshold for memory management in long-running stores

Tests (7 new):
- ssi_detects_write_skew: Classic A-reads-X/writes-Y, B-reads-Y/writes-X
  pattern correctly rejected
- ssi_allows_read_only_txn: Read-only txns never trigger SSI rejection
- ssi_allows_disjoint_read_write_sets: Non-overlapping R/W across blocks OK
- ssi_fcw_layer_still_active: Write-write conflicts still caught by FCW
- ssi_log_pruning: Prune correctly retains only entries above threshold
- lab_ssi_rejects_write_skew: 20-seed deterministic lab runtime test verifying
  exactly one of two write-skew txns is rejected and sum invariant holds (a+b=3)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
